content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 5, 2), (2, 3, 3), (1, 3, 4), (5, 7, 8), (0, 1, 9), (4, 5, 10), (6, 7, 12), (5, 8, 14), (0, 5, 15), (1, 6, 16), (1, 8, 16), (3, 8, 17), (1, 2, 24), (1, 9, 24), (2, 8, 28), (6, 9, 29)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 6 exactly at time 29.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

For time 29:

* Edge (6, 7, 12) has node 6 and time 12. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (5, 8, 14) has node 6 but time is 14 (not 29). -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 5, 15) has node 6 but time is 15 (not 29). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 6, 16) has node 6 and time 16. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (1, 8, 16) has node 6 and time 16. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (3, 8, 17) has node 6 but time is 17 (not 29). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 2, 24) has node 6 but time is 24 (not 29). -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 9, 24) has node 6 but time is 24 (not 29). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 8, 28) has node 6 but time is 28 (not 29). -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 9, 29) has node 6 and time 29. -> Add node 9.

The nodes matched are 7, 8, and 9.

Answer: [7, 8, 9]
